item.title: India proposes making government advisories legally binding on tech giants
item.description: India on Tuesday proposed changes to its IT law to make advisories ​and clarifications legally binding on internet platforms ‌such as Meta , Google and X, the latest in a string of stricter compliance requirements for tech ​giants.

item.title: Global bond prices set for biggest monthly fall in years as Iran war stokes stagflation fears
item.description: Global government bond prices were set for ​their biggest monthly fall in years as investors weighed the risks from a prolonged war in the Middle East on inflation and growth.

item.title: Air Canada CEO Michael Rousseau to retire
item.description: Air Canada ‌said on Monday CEO Michael Rousseau will ​retire by ​the end of the ⁠third quarter after ​nearly two decades ​with the Canadian flag carrier.

item.title: New Nasdaq rules to include 'fast entry' for new listings on benchmark index
item.description: Nasdaq ​will roll out a set of rules including steps to speed up the entry of newly listed large-cap companies to its Nasdaq-100 index, as ‌the exchange operator seeks to reduce delays in joining the flagship equity benchmark.

item.title: Lavazza posts higher sales, profit despite surging coffee prices and weak demand
item.description: Italian coffee maker Lavazza on Monday posted an increase ​in annual revenue and profit ‌despite persistently high bean prices and weaker global demand.

item.title: U.S. dividend funds draw strong flows as investors seek safety
item.description: U.S. dividend income funds are attracting strong flows this year as investors seek shelter from geopolitical risks and opt for stable, income-generating equities.

item.title: Swiss cling on to cash as survey shows payment app use stalling
item.description: Mobile payment app usage stalled in Switzerland last year, a Swiss National Bank survey showed on Monday, with cash remaining popular ​as a way of paying for goods and services in ‌person.

item.title: Starcloud reaches $1.1 billion valuation as AI space race heats up
item.description: Orbital compute infrastructure startup Starcloud has raised $170 million at a $1.1 billion ‌valuation, as companies including Elon Musk's SpaceX and Jeff Bezos' Blue Origin race to move power-hungry AI data centers off-planet.

item.title: Airbus Helicopters CEO expects oil rally to accelerate H225 comeback
item.description: Soaring oil ​prices could boost offshore development and accelerate a comeback of the H225 Super Puma, 10 years after a safety crisis, ‌the outgoing head of Airbus Helicopters said.

item.title: Morning Bid: Crude escalation
item.description: What matters in U.S. and global markets today

item.title: Key milestones in NASA's Artemis moon program
item.description: NASA's Artemis program is the U.S. effort to return astronauts to the moon for the first time since the Apollo era and eventually establish a sustained human presence there, a goal Washington has framed as central to maintaining space leadership amid growing competition from China. Here are key milestones in ​the Artemis program:

item.title: Sysco nears $29 billion deal to buy Restaurant Depot, WSJ reports
item.description: Food ​distributor Sysco is nearing ‌a $29 billion deal to acquire catering supplier Restaurant Depot, ​including debt, the ​Wall Street Journal reported ⁠on Monday.

item.title: How NASA's Artemis II moon mission will unfold
item.description: NASA's Artemis II mission is expected to last about 10 days, sending four astronauts on a high-speed journey around the moon and back in the first crewed lunar mission since the ​Apollo era. Artemis II is designed to pave the way for future missions aimed ‌at returning astronauts to the lunar surface later this decade.

item.title: On the horns of an inflation-growth dilemma, Fed Chair Powell to speak at Harvard
item.description: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell speaks on Monday at an introductory macroeconomics class at Harvard ​University, where his remarks will draw scrutiny for how he may answer the classic textbook question: how does a central bank respond ‌to a shock that simultaneously feeds inflation and slows the economy?

item.title: US airlines face fuel-driven financial shakeout
item.description: When United Airlines CEO Scott Kirby wrote to employees about the oil price surge earlier this month, the most telling line was not about fuel bills or flight cuts. It ​was about opportunity.

item.title: Fed's faith in anchored inflation expectations may be coming under stress
item.description: Federal Reserve officials eager to keep inflation psychology in check and maintain control over prices face a challenge as household expectations rise alongside the cost of gasoline and doubt edges into bond markets in the form of rising yields on U.S. Treasury securities.

item.title: US stock futures edge up after selloff as Mideast conflict remains in focus
item.description: U.S. stock index futures kicked off a holiday-shortened week ​with gains on Monday, after the indexes logged sharp declines in the previous session, as investors assessed the impact ‌of the escalating Middle East conflict.

item.title: Nvidia's PE sinks to seven-year low as war and AI angst weigh
item.description: As global stock markets tumble over deepening worries about war in the Middle East, Nvidia, the world's most valuable company, finds itself trading at its cheapest price-to-earnings multiple ​since before ChatGPT kicked off the AI boom.

item.title: US menus change as Trump's tariffs hit wine prices
item.description: Certain champagne and cremant brands that were once wine menu staples are on the chopping block at restaurants and bars ​owned by New York-based Kent Hospitality Group, says wine director Kristen Goceljak. Tariffs have made them too expensive.

item.title: BOJ highlights inflationary pressure from oil, weak yen
item.description: Japan's underlying inflation rate may face stronger upward pressure from rising oil prices ‌and the yen's declines than in the past as firms have become more active in hiking prices, the Bank of Japan said on Monday.

item.title: Taiwan probes 11 Chinese firms for illegal poaching of tech talent
item.description: Taiwan said on Monday 11 Chinese firms are being investigated for alleged illegal poaching of semiconductor and other high‑tech ​talent, stepping up efforts to curb technology outflows amid rising ‌geopolitical tensions with Beijing.

item.title: WTO suffers fresh blow as reform push hits a wall at Cameroon meeting
item.description: World Trade Organization talks broke up on Monday with no agreement on a plan for reform or even on extending a moratorium on e-commerce, piling more pressure on the trade body that finds itself increasingly sidelined ​by economic nationalism.

item.title: Morgan Stanley downgrades global equities; sees US as 'defensive' market amid Mideast conflict
item.description: Morgan Stanley downgraded global equities and upgraded cash and U.S. government bonds, as investors shun risk in favor of safe-haven assets due ​to mounting uncertainty stemming from the Middle East war.

item.title: US consumers, importers take biggest hit from tariffs, ECB study finds
item.description: U.S. consumers and importers take the vast majority of the financial hit from tariffs ​but trade volumes also suffer, resulting in a ‌negative shock for exporters, too, a European Central Bank Economic Bulletin article said on Monday.

item.title: China's factory activity seen returning to expansion in March: Reuters poll
item.description: China's factory activity likely expanded in March - snapping a two-month contraction - amid strong momentum in goods exports, although supply chain ​shocks from the Iran war cloud the outlook.

item.title: China's DeepSeek AI chatbot suffers longest outage since viral rise in early 2025
item.description: China's popular DeepSeek artificial intelligence chatbot suffered on Monday its longest outage since ​the viral rise of its flagship R1 ‌and V3 models early last year.

item.title: Airlines face fare dilemma as fuel spike threatens travel demand
item.description: Global airlines have begun to hike fares and cut capacity to cope with the sudden surge in the oil price, but the industry's ability to remain profitable may depend on whether consumers pull back on ​flying as gasoline costs threaten household budgets.

item.title: Rio Tinto resumes operations at three Pilbara port terminals after cyclone Narelle
item.description: Rio Tinto said on Monday operations at three ​of its four Pilbara iron ore port ‌terminals have resumed after Tropical Cyclone Narelle swept through Western Australia's Pilbara region, disrupting shipments but leaving its ​annual guidance unchanged.

item.title: France's Mistral raises $830 million in debt for AI data centre build-up
item.description: Europe's leading AI provider Mistral has raised $830 million in new ​debt to buy 13,800 Nvidia chips for a major ‌data centre near Paris, the firm told Reuters, as Europe races to scale AI infrastructure to compete with the U.S. and China.

item.title: Luxury carmakers' gold-leafed Gulf profits under threat from Iran war
item.description: A laser-engraved hood inspired by Arabian architecture and a matching wood-trim interior are among the luxuries Rolls-Royce featured in a one-off Phantom Arabesque model commissioned by a Dubai customer it showcased in February before ​the Iran war.
